name: Mountain-Style Paella
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds breakfast pork sausage links, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 pound green beans, trimmed and cut into 1-inch lengths
2 cups arborio rice, or short-grain white rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut 1 bell pepper lengthwise into thin strips. Chop the second pepper; reserve.
2. Heat oil in a heavy 14-inch skillet or paella pan over medium-high heat. Add bell pepper strips and sauté until softened, about 6 minutes. Using tongs, transfer pepper strips to a bowl; reserve.
3. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Add to the skillet and cook until brown on all sides, about 12 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
4. Add sausage to the skillet and sauté until golden, about 5 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to the plate with the chicken.
5. Pour off all but 6 tablespoons drippings from the skillet. Add mushrooms; saute over medium-high heat for 5 minutes.
6. Stir in tomatoes, garlic, and reserved chopped bell pepper. Bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer until almost all liquid evaporates, about 35 minutes.
7. Add green beans and the next 7 ingredients to the skillet. Stir in rice, chicken, sausage, and 6 cups hot broth; bring to boil.
8. Reduce heat to medium and simmer uncovered until chicken is cooked, adding more broth if the mixture seems dry, about 30 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes.
9. Top with pepper strips. Garnish with lemon.
Notes:
About two centuries ago, field workers near Valencia began sharing a luncheon dish made of rice, vegetables, snails, and eels cooked in a flat, oval, two-handled metal pan known as a paellera. And so paella was born. Rice dishes made in such a pan, whatever the ingredients, are called paella or just arroz con ("rice with"), followed by a description of the contents. The traditional components of this particular version are snails, rabbit, and rosemary, which are found in the Valencian mountains.
This equally delectable rendition omits the snails and substitutes chicken for rabbit. This recipe can be made in a paella pan, a flameproof earthenware casserole, or a large skillet.
